subject: LU-7734 lnet: Multi-Rail local NI split

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 3:

(8 comments)

First pass, more comments may follow later.

I added some notes on why certain code is in lnet_parse_networks(). Getting a parser to do exactly the right thing in all cases is hard.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

I still have to address Olaf's comments on config.c
I've just been trying to get the regression tests passing. Then will move to that.

(1 comment)

(1 comment)

Patch Set 4:

(5 comments)

None of my comments warrant a -1, but are observations of things we should consider fixing in the future to make this code more maintainable.  

However, the Maloo failures show there is a mismatch of NI addref / decref calls causing problems.  I will investigate fixing that.  As such, I am not giving either a +1 or -1 as this patch will need to be changed.

commit.subject: LU-7734 lnet: Multi-Rail local NI split
commit.message:
LU-7734 lnet: Multi-Rail local NI split

This patch allows the configuration of multiple NIs under one Net.
It is now possible to have multiple NIDs on the same network:
   Ex: <ip1>@tcp, <ip2>@tcp.
This can be configured using the following syntax:
   Ex: tcp(eth0, eth1)

The data structures for the example above can be visualized
as follows

               NET(tcp)
                |
        -----------------
        |               |
      NI(eth0)        NI(eth1)

For more details refer to the Mult-Rail Requirements and HLD
documents

Signed-off-by: Amir Shehata <amir.shehata@intel.com>
Change-Id: Id7c73b9b811a3082b61e53b9e9f95743188cbd51
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/18274
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Olaf Weber <olaf@sgi.com>
